description: Dash chases fireflies through a summer night while explaining bioluminescence and why flashing is a signal — not just a pretty light.
thumbnail_concept: Night meadow gradient; Dash mid-run reaching for a bright firefly; trails of yellow-green dashes; title: 'WHY FIREFLIES GLOW'; high readability, 9:16.
voice_direction: Playful chase energy, whisper-wonder on chemistry line, clear on 'signaling'; smile audible without being childish.
music_direction: Light nocturnal pizzicato + soft pads; twinkle motifs synced to flashes; playful chase percussion under run; gentle settle on payoff.
scientific_notes: Firefly light is bioluminescence (luciferin/luciferase chemistry in light organs).
Flashing patterns often function in mating signaling; also discussed in warning/communication contexts depending on species.
Do not claim all fireflies use identical codes — keep general.

script.hook: Fireflies don't just glow for decoration — they're sending messages.
body: That light is chemistry: a tiny reaction in their bodies that releases energy as a flash.
Different patterns can mean different signals — like a secret code in the dark.
Often, it's about finding a mate.
Sometimes the glow also says: I'm here… pay attention.
script.payoff: So every flash is more than pretty — it's a conversation written in light.
script.full_text: Fireflies don't just glow for decoration — they're sending messages. That light is chemistry: a tiny reaction in their bodies that releases energy as a flash. Different patterns can mean different signals — like a secret code in the dark. Often, it's about finding a mate. Sometimes the glow also says: I'm here… pay attention. So every flash is more than pretty — it's a conversation written in light.

scenes.narration: Sometimes the glow also says: I'm here… pay attention.
